REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDERS GRANTING MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER AND DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IS DENIED AS DEFENDANTS HAVE FAILED TO SHOW ANY NEW FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES OR LAW THAT WERE NOT OR COULD NOT HAVE BEEN PRESENTED TO THE COURT AT THE TIME OF THE ORIGINAL HEARING. THE BASIS FOR THE MOTION APPEARS TO BE ENTRY OF ORDERS REPEATING THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM 3 WEEKS AFTER THE HEARING AND 2 WEEKS AFTER THE ORDERS WERE LODGED VIA E-FILING WITH THE COURT INDICATING THAT SUCH PROPOSED ORDERS HAVE BEEN PREVIOUSLY SERVED ON THE DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT. MOVING PARTY APPEARS TO ADMIT THAT THE MOTION TO STRIKE AND THE DEMURRER WERE NOT TIMELY OPPOSED; THAT NOT TIMELY NOTICE TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS PROVIDED; AND THAT NO TIMELY AMENDED PLEADINGS HAVE BEEN FILED. HOWEVER MOVING PARTY DOES NOT SEEK ANY RELIEF IN CONJUNCTION WITH THESE ADMISSIONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Answer And First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-28-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Gregory Walston; 415-956-9200; The Walston Law Group; counsel for plaintiff appearing via Zoom.
Micah Nash; 650-241-9189; Etek Lawk; counsel for defendant appearing via Zoom.

Parties do not contest the court's tentative but defendant seeks clarification on whether motion was denied because the entire answer was stricken or if only parts of the answer were stricken. The court rules as follows:

DEFENDANT RAYDIANT, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, MORGAN CHANEY MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Answer And First Amended Cross-Complaint is DENIED as moot because PARTS of the answer was stricken and a demurrer to the cross-complaint was sustained without leave to amend.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Motion For Reconsideration Of Orders Granting Motion To Strike Answer And Demurrer To Cross Complaint Without Leave To Amend
Off Calendar Oct-28-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Gregory Walston; 415-956-9200; The Walston Law Group; counsel for plaintiff appearing via Zoom.
Micah Nash; 650-241-9189; Etek Lawk; counsel for defendant appearing via Zoom.

Not contested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RAYDIANT, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, MORGAN CHANEY Motion For Reconsideration Of Orders Granting Motion To Strike Answer And Demurrer To Cross Complaint Without Leave To Amend is DENIED as Defendants have failed to show any new facts, circumstances or law that were not or could not have been presented to the Court at the time of the original hearing. The basis for the motion appears to be entry of orders repeating the tentative ruling verbatim 3 weeks after the hearing and 2 weeks after the orders were lodged via e-filing with the Court indicating that such proposed orders have been previously served on the defendant/cross-complainant. Moving party appears to admit that the Motion to Strike and the Demurrer were not timely opposed; that not timely notice to contest the tentative ruling was provided; and that no timely amended pleadings have been filed. However moving party does not seek any relief in conjunction with these admissions.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
